newproject.title: A new project takes shape
newproject.modeLabel: Graphics mode (decides how the C64 reads your pixels):
newproject.mode.textHires.hint: Crisp single pixels, 2 colours per character — coming later.
newproject.mode.textMulticolor.hint: Wide double-pixels, 4 colours per character — the good-natured tilemap default.
newproject.mode.bitmapMulticolor.hint: Pixel the whole screen freely, RAM-hungry — coming later.
newproject.boilerplate: Add a starter scaffold to main.crumb (setup + frame loop)
newproject.regionLabel: Region (sets the pace and the frame budget):
newproject.region.pal.hint: A touch more room per frame. The European default, and where most of the retro scene lives.
newproject.region.ntsc.hint: Faster, but a tighter frame budget — a game that just fits on PAL can overrun here.
toolbar.editorNeedsProject: Create or open a project first
toolbar.prompt.newFileName: Name of the new file (e.g. enemies.crumb):
build.viceHint: Tip: set the VICE path in the global settings, then the program starts automatically.
settings.title: Global settings
settings.startup.label: On startup
settings.emulator.title: Emulator (VICE)
settings.emulator.intro: Path to the VICE executable ({exe}). This lets BreadCraft test a built program directly in the emulator until the embedded emulator is available.
settings.emulator.notVice: ⚠ File found, but does not look like {exe}.
settings.language.title: Language
settings.language.label: Interface language
setup.label: First-time setup
setup.title: Set up the workspace folder
setup.intro: BreadCraft stores its projects here. Two folders are created: {temp} for temporary projects and {projects} for your permanent ones.
explorer.title: Project explorer
explorer.prompt.newFileName: Name of the new file (e.g. enemies.crumb):
explorer.prompt.newAssetName: Name (without extension, e.g. player or blob):
saveas.newFolderName: Name of the new folder (e.g. assets/sprites):
view.sound.title: Sound converter
view.tilemap.title: Tilemap editor
view.tileset.title: PETSCII tiles editor
view.palette.title: Palette editor
palette.subtitle: Define your game's colors
palette.intro: The Commodore 64 has only 16 fixed colors. Pick the few your whole game shares here — every tile and every sprite uses them.
palette.hiresNote: In hi-res mode each character has only two colours — the background here, plus one free colour per cell. The shared multicolor colours don't exist here, so the editor hides them.
palette.pickHint: Select a slot above, then click here — or drag a color straight onto a slot.
palette.slot.background.label: Background
palette.slot.background.hint: The base color behind everything — fills the whole screen.
palette.slot.shared1.label: Shared color 1
palette.slot.shared1.hint: A color shared by all tiles and all sprites.
palette.slot.shared2.label: Shared color 2
palette.slot.shared2.hint: Another shared color for all tiles and all sprites.
font.hint: Crisp hi-res text: 8×8, one pixel per dot. The colour is set in-game by the Color command.
tileset.lightTableTitle: Show the previous tile (N−1) as a colour ghost beneath the pixels — to align animation phases
tileset.counterTitle: Used tile slots (64–255). {font} slots are reserved for the Hires font.
tileset.props.title: Tile {n}
tileset.props.solidBrush: Brush: paint solid across the grid (right-drag clears)
tileset.metaSoon: Here you'll combine characters into larger tiles — soon.
sprite.newSpriteTitle: Start a fresh, empty canvas — nothing is saved until you choose Save as
sprite.newDiscardWarn: The current sprite has unsaved changes. Start a new one and discard them?
sprite.addFrameTitle: Another picture of the same figure — what animation is made of
sprite.removeFrameTitle: Remove this picture (the last one stays — a figure needs at least one)
tilemap.colorRamHint: The fourth, free colour — chosen per cell (Color-RAM).
tilemap.clearTitle: Empty the whole map (reset to blank Space)
tilemap.clearConfirm: Empty the whole map? This cannot be undone.
tilemap.tilesHint: Pick a tile, then paint it onto the map.
tilemap.noCharset: Paint some characters in the PETSCII editor first — those are the tiles here.
view.tilemap.empty: No map yet. Pick a tileset to begin.
view.sprite.title: Sprite editor
view.sprite.empty: No sprite yet. Create a new 24×21 sprite to begin.
welcome.recentEmpty: No projects yet. Create one to begin.
outliner.title: Outliner
outliner.empty: No functions. Function definitions appear here.
console.title: Output
console.empty: No output yet. Build & Run shows its messages here.
health.ram.meta: no build yet · cc65 map provides the value
health.perf.meta: no build yet · rough estimate from the code
health.perf.fullHint: your logic doesn't fit in one frame → VWait silently halves it to 25 fps
intellisense.plannedDoc: This word already belongs to the language, but its C64 code isn't wired up yet. Reach for it today and the build hands you an honest "comes later" — not a mystery.
docs.guide.crumb-sprache: CRUMB — a language of your own
docs.cheatsheet.title: Cheat sheet
docs.cheatsheet.subtitle: All commands, functions, keywords and operators at a glance. Click a card for the detailed page.
docs.cost.label: Cost
